name: J. Cole
description:
'''Jermaine Lamarr Cole''' (born January 28, 1985) is an American rapper, singer, songwriter and record producer. Born on a military base in Germany and raised in [[Fayetteville, North Carolina]], Cole initially gained attention as a rapper following the release of his debut mixtape, ''[[The Come Up]]'', in early 2007. He attended college in [[New York City]] with intent in furthering his musical career, and signed with [[Jay-Z]]'s [[Roc Nation]] in 2009; the label  released his next two mixtapes: ''[[The Warm Up]]'' (2009) and ''[[Friday Night Lights (mixtape)|Friday Night Lights]]'' (2010), both of which earned him wider recognition.

Each of Cole's studio albums have peaked atop the US [[Billboard 200|''Billboard'' 200]], beginning with his debut, ''[[Cole World: The Sideline Story]]'' (2011), and its follow-up, ''[[Born Sinner]]'' (2013). Both met with critical acclaim, the albums spawned the [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]]-top 40 singles "[[Work Out (J. Cole song)|Work Out]]", "[[Power Trip (song)|Power Trip]]" (featuring [[Miguel (singer)|Miguel]]), and "[[Crooked Smile]]" (featuring [[TLC (band)|TLC]]). His subsequent albums—beginning with ''[[2014 Forest Hills Drive]]'' (2014), ''[[4 Your Eyez Only]]'' (2016), and ''[[KOD (album)|KOD]]'' (2018)—have since explored more [[Conscious hip-hop|conscious]] and introspective subject matter; the latter featured a then-record six simultaneous top 20 hits on the ''Billboard'' Hot 100—the first time an album had done so since [[the Beatles]] in 1964. His sixth and seventh albums, ''[[The Off-Season]]'' (2021) and ''[[The Fall-Off]]'' (2026; marketed as his final album), both had several top-40 entries upon release, with the former's "[[My Life (J. Cole, 21 Savage and Morray song)|My Life]]" (with [[21 Savage]] and [[Morray]]) peaking at number two on the ''Billboard'' Hot 100. His guest appearances on the 2023 singles "[[All My Life (Lil Durk song)|All My Life]]" by [[Lil Durk]] and "[[First Person Shooter (Drake song)|First Person Shooter]]" by [[Drake (entertainer)|Drake]], peaked at numbers two and one on the chart, respectively.

Self-taught on piano, Cole also acts as a [[Hip-hop production|producer]] alongside his recording career—having largely handled the production of his own projects—with credits on material for other artists, including [[Kendrick Lamar]], [[Janet Jackson]], [[Young Thug]], [[Wale (rapper)|Wale]], and [[Mac Miller]], among others. Cole's side ventures include his record label [[Dreamville Records]], as well as its [[Dreamville Ventures|namesake media company]] and [[Dreamville Records#The Dreamville Foundation|non-profit]]. The label, having signed artists including [[JID]], [[Ari Lennox]], [[Bas (rapper)|Bas]], and [[EarthGang]], has released four compilation albums; their third, ''[[Revenge of the Dreamers III]]'' (2019), debuted atop the ''Billboard'' 200 and was nominated for Best Rap Album at the [[62nd Annual Grammy Awards]]. In January 2015, Cole began housing single mothers rent-free at his childhood home in Fayetteville.

Cole has won two [[Grammy Award]]s from seventeen nominations, a [[Billboard Music Award|''Billboard'' Music Award]] for [[Billboard Music Award for Top Rap Album|Top Rap Album]], three [[Soul Train Music Awards]], and eight [[BET Hip Hop Awards]]. Each of his albums—including ''Revenge of the Dreamers III''—have received [[RIAA certification#List of certifications|platinum]] certifications by the [[Recording Industry Association of America]] (RIAA). He is a minority owner of the [[Charlotte Hornets]].
